hook: AI gave you a 47-step plan when you asked a simple question. Here's why.
caption:
AI gave you a 47-step plan when you asked a simple question. Here's why.

You typed: "How should I approach this difficult conversation with my coworker?"

AI responded with:
— Acknowledge their feelings
— Use 'I' statements
— Choose a neutral location
— Schedule a time when neither of you is stressed
— Prepare talking points in advance
— Consider the power dynamic
— [41 more bullet points you'll never read]

You closed the tab.

Here's what actually happened: the AI doesn't know you're someone who needs the 20-second version, not the MBA seminar.

It doesn't know you process by doing, not planning. That you'd rather have one sharp sentence you can actually say out loud than a framework with sub-frameworks.

So it defaulted to "comprehensive" — which is just AI for "I don't know you well enough to edit."

The fix isn't a better prompt. It's a profile.

When AI knows you're a bottom-line thinker who hates over-explanation, it stops padding. It gives you the sentence. Just the sentence.

That's what bootfile.ai builds — a permanent instruction profile based on how YOUR brain actually works. So every AI interaction starts already knowing your style.

No more 47-step plans for a one-step problem.
